mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Range Rover Sport SVR engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Range Rover Sport SVR (AJ133 5.0 SC V8) engine typically costs £5,500–£9,500 supply only. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,800 at independent specialist rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Range Rover Sport SVR engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ133 5.0-litre supercharged V8 delivers epic performance and a glorious soundtrack. But it has three well-documented weak points: timing chain tensioner failure, plastic coolant pipe failure, and oil consumption. Properly maintained (frequent oil changes, aluminium coolant pipes fitted), it can be reliable; neglected, it becomes very expensive.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Range Rover Sport SVR?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain tensioner failure (cold-start rattle is the early warning), plastic coolant pipe failure in the 'V' of the engine (Land Rover now offers aluminium replacements), excessive oil consumption (1L per 1,000 miles in some cases), and supercharger seal leaks. All are manageable with preventative maintenance and early detection.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Range Rover Sport SVR engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Against a well-maintained SVR (values £35,000–£70,000+ depending on year and mileage), a £6,000–£8,000 reconditioned engine is 10–25% of the vehicle's value — worth it. The Land Rover scrap threshold of 60% means replacement is almost always viable on an SVR, given the model's strong enthusiast following and residual values.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Range Rover Sport SVR engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code AJ133 is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The SVR was offered on the L494 from 2014–2022 (550PS 2014–2017, 575PS 2017–2022) and returned on the L461 in 2024 with a BMW-sourced 4.4TT V8. If your L494 is an SVR, it's the AJ133. Check your V5C or VIN plate for confirmation.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the timing chain, or does it require engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain tensioner failure is repairable without replacing the engine — if caught early. A preventative chain and tensioner replacement costs £1,200–£2,500. If the chain skips and valves meet pistons, a full engine replacement (£5,500–£9,500) is usually required. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an SVR engine typically takes 14–20 hours at £75–£110/hr (independent) or £130–£180/hr (London/SE dealer). The supercharger and V8 layout add complexity. [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]

problems.title: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure (AJ133)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start lasting 1–5 seconds, engine warning light, loss of power, metallic noises from the front of the engine.
problems.replacementCost: £5,500–£9,500 (if chain skips and valves meet pistons)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The AJ133's plastic timing chain tensioners are the single biggest reliability concern on the SVR. Extended oil service intervals (Land Rover's 10,000-mile recommendation is too long for a hot V8) accelerate wear on the plastic guides and tensioners. A cold-start rattle is the early warning sign — ignore it and you risk a £7,000+ engine replacement. Replace the full timing chain kit (chains, tensioners, guides, sprockets) as a complete assembly, not just the chain alone.

problems.title: Coolant Pipe Failure (AJ133)
problems.symptoms: Sudden coolant loss, steam from bonnet, overheating warning, sweet smell from engine bay, low coolant warning with no visible external leak.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (unless overheating leads to head gasket or engine damage)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if coolant loss is sudden, stop driving immediately

problems.recommendation: The AJ133 has rigid plastic coolant pipes that sit in the "V" underneath the supercharger. These have moulded joins that can fail, often without warning. If failure happens at speed, the coolant can drain before you've even pulled over. Land Rover has released aluminium front and rear coolant pipes to address this known issue. If your SVR hasn't had this upgrade, consider it preventative maintenance — the aluminium pipes are a permanent fix.

problems.title: Oil Consumption & Starvation (AJ133)
problems.symptoms: Low oil warning light, needing to top up oil frequently (1L per 1,000 miles or more), ticking noise from engine, eventual knocking from bottom end.
problems.repairCost: £150–£300 (regular oil top-ups and monitoring) — prevention is key
problems.replacementCost: £5,500–£9,500 (if oil starvation leads to spun bearings and bottom-end failure)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if oil consumption is excessive

problems.recommendation: Many AJ133 engines burn 1L of oil per 1,000 miles — not inherently fatal, but if ignored, low oil leads to spun rod bearings and total bottom-end failure. Land Rover issued TSB LTB00485 (2015) acknowledging "excessive oil consumption under spirited driving conditions" but offered no free fix. Check your oil level weekly — this is not an engine you can neglect. Use the correct grade of fully synthetic oil and consider reducing oil change intervals to 6,000–8,000 miles.

problems.title: Supercharger Seal Leaks (AJ133)
problems.symptoms: Oil leak from the front of the supercharger, belt contamination, squealing from auxiliary belt, loss of supercharger efficiency.

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon; can lead to supercharger seizure

problems.recommendation: Oil leaks from the supercharger nose seal can contaminate belts and cause slippage or failure — often misdiagnosed as "just a leak" until the supercharger seizes. If you notice oil around the front of the supercharger or a squealing belt, get it inspected. A nose seal rebuild is far cheaper than a new supercharger.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Range Rover Sport SVR — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — far cheaper than catastrophic failure
Replace with aluminium pipes for permanent fix
Preventative maintenance is essential; rebuild if bearings are damaged
Piston-valve contact means little is left to repair

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Range Rover Sport SVR? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with oil changes at 6,000–8,000 mile intervals (not JLR's extended schedule)
* Aluminium coolant pipes fitted (or proof of recent plastic pipe replacement)
* No cold-start rattle — timing chain tensioners should be healthy
* Supercharger functioning correctly with no oil leaks or belt squeal
avoidIf: * No proof of regular oil changes — this V8 is unforgiving of neglect
* Cold-start rattle present — timing chain work is imminent
* Coolant loss with no diagnosis — plastic pipes may be failing
* Any engine warning light without diagnosis — the AJ133 can hide expensive faults

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Range Rover Sport SVR is a landmark performance SUV — JLR's first Special Vehicle Operations model, with a soundtrack and presence that few rivals can match. The AJ133 5.0-litre supercharged V8 is a genuinely special engine, but it demands respect: timing chain tensioners, plastic coolant pipes, and oil consumption are the three critical weak points. Buy on service history, budget for preventative maintenance (especially the aluminium coolant pipes), and this is one of the most rewarding SUVs ever built. Neglect it, and the bills will be eye-watering.

cta.label: → Compare Range Rover Sport SVR engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Range Rover Sport SVR (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattle lasting more than 1–2 seconds (timing chain tensioner warning)
* Service History — Oil changes every 6,000–8,000 miles are essential for this high-performance V8
* Coolant Level — Check cold and hot; any loss indicates potential coolant pipe issues
* Oil Level — Check dipstick; excessive consumption indicates potential oil starvation risk
* Supercharger — Any oil leaks from the nose seal or belt squeal?
* Smoke Check — Blue smoke = oil burning; black smoke = overfuelling
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Timing, coolant, or oil-related codes are most common
* MOT History — Check for emissions failures (often related to fuelling or catalytic converter issues)

items.label: SECTION 8 — FAQ
items.value: 1. How much does a Land Rover Range Rover Sport SVR engine replacement cost in the UK?

items.value: 2. Is the Land Rover Range Rover Sport SVR engine reliable?

But it has three well-documented weak points: timing chain tensioner failure, plastic coolant pipe failure, and oil consumption. Properly maintained (frequent oil changes, aluminium coolant pipes fitted), it can be reliable; neglected, it becomes very expensive.
items.value: 3. What are the common problems with the Range Rover Sport SVR?

All are manageable with preventative maintenance and early detection.
items.value: 4. Is it worth replacing a Range Rover Sport SVR engine, or should I scrap the car?

items.label: Against a well-maintained SVR (values £35,000–£70,000+ depending on year and mileage), a £6,000–£8,000 reconditioned engine is 10–25% of the vehicle's value — worth it. The Land Rover scrap threshold of 60% means replacement is almost always viable on an SVR, given the model's strong enthusiast following and residual values.
items.value: 5. How do I find my Range Rover Sport SVR engine code?

items.label: The engine code AJ133 is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The SVR was offered on the L494 from 2014–2022 (550PS 2014–2017, 575PS 2017–2022) and returned on the L461 in 2024 with a BMW-sourced 4.4TT V8. If your L494 is an SVR, it's the AJ133. Check your V5C or VIN plate for confirmation.
items.value: 6. Can I repair the timing chain, or does it require engine replacement?

items.label: Timing chain tensioner failure is repairable without replacing the engine — if caught early. A preventative chain and tensioner replacement costs £1,200–£2,500. If the chain skips and valves meet pistons, a full engine replacement (£5,500–£9,500) is usually required. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.
items.value: Disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data from our network of engine specialists. Actual costs vary by variant, engine code, damage severity and workshop location. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes.
